page: Microsoft Azure D96s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, with 2.0 threads per core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled, though the instance is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core compute capabilities and high throughput. The server achieved a top-tier PassMark CPU score of 85,698.46 and a top-tier extended instructions score of 106,181.55. Multi-core performance is further highlighted by a strong stress-ng multi-core score of 81,378.95 and a strong Geekbench multi-core score of 17,846.0. In memory performance, cached small-block read bandwidth is strong at 6,201.91 GB/sec, though uncached large-block read and write speeds are average, and the PassMark memory score is categorized as weak. The instance demonstrates top-tier static web serving throughput of 114.52 GB/sec and strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 460.38 GB/sec. Large language model inference speeds are strong for prompt processing across small, medium, and large models, while text generation speeds range from average to strong.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D96s_v6 offers a balanced resource profile suitable for enterprise-scale workloads that require high compute density without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The absence of bundled local storage means storage costs are decoupled, allowing users to provision Premium Storage as needed. Given its strong multi-core processing, high web serving throughput, and robust database operation benchmarks, this server is well-suited for high-traffic web serving, software compilation, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv6 family, powered by a dedicated 96-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with 48 physical cores, it features 384.0 GB of RAM, a 260 MB L3 cache, and supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. The server does not include bundled local storage or GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in PassMark CPU tests and static web serving throughput, alongside strong multi-core processing and database operations. However, uncached memory bandwidth remains average, and its PassMark memory score is weak. This configuration provides a balanced, cost-efficient resource profile for enterprise workloads. It is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM prompt processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v6 is a general-purpose VM featuring 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering top-tier CPU performance and static web serving throughput.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v6 is a 96-vCPU general-purpose VM with 384 GB RAM, offering top-tier CPU benchmarks and strong multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-density, 96-vCPU general-purpose virtual machine delivering top-tier compute performance and strong multi-threaded processing for enterprise workloads.

384.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core.
Top-tier PassMark CPU performance and static web serving throughput.
Supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX.
No bundled local storage, allowing flexible Premium Storage configuration.
Best suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and database operations.
